vocabulary.meaning: belonging to the very distant past and no longer in existence
vocabulary.example: We visited the ruins of an ancient temple in Greece.

vocabulary.meaning: patterned with alternating squares of different colors
vocabulary.example: He wore a red and black checked flannel shirt.

vocabulary.meaning: a soft white fibrous substance used to make textile fabrics and clothes
vocabulary.example: This t-shirt is made of 100% organic cotton.

vocabulary.meaning: to bring something into existence through creative effort
vocabulary.example: The artist wanted to create a unique sculpture for the exhibition.

vocabulary.meaning: to plan and make a drawing of something, or a plan/drawing showing the look and function of something
vocabulary.example: She designs custom wedding dresses. / The new building design is very modern.

vocabulary.meaning: to repair or mend something that is broken
vocabulary.example: Can you fix my broken bicycle chain?

vocabulary.meaning: to bend something over on itself so that one part of it covers another
vocabulary.example: Please fold the laundry and put it in the drawers.

vocabulary.meaning: a room or building for the display or sale of works of art
vocabulary.example: We went to the national gallery to see the new art exhibition.

vocabulary.meaning: an instance of making or becoming better; progress
vocabulary.example: There has been a significant improvement in his test scores.

vocabulary.meaning: not firmly or tightly fixed in place; baggier or comfortable
vocabulary.example: He wore a loose t-shirt because it was hot outside.

vocabulary.meaning: to cause or enable a state or situation to continue
vocabulary.example: You must maintain a steady speed on the highway.

vocabulary.meaning: to correspond or be equal to in quality, color, or design
vocabulary.example: These shoes don't match your dress.

vocabulary.meaning: the physical matter from which a thing is or can be made
vocabulary.example: Wood is a durable material for making furniture.

vocabulary.meaning: to observe or pay attention to, or information/warning given in advance
vocabulary.example: I noticed a small mistake in the report. / The manager gave two weeks' notice before leaving.

vocabulary.meaning: a repeated decorative design
vocabulary.example: The wallpaper has a beautiful floral pattern.

vocabulary.meaning: a heap of things laid or lying one on top of another
vocabulary.example: There was a large pile of books on the desk.

vocabulary.meaning: suitable for actual use rather than theoretical or artistic purposes
vocabulary.example: She wore practical shoes suitable for walking in the mud.

vocabulary.meaning: having an uneven or irregular surface; not smooth
vocabulary.example: The bark of the old tree was rough and dry.

vocabulary.meaning: the external form or outline of someone or something
vocabulary.example: The cookie cutter shapes the dough into stars.

vocabulary.meaning: a fine, strong, soft lustrous fiber produced by silkworms
vocabulary.example: She bought a beautiful silk scarf from the market.

vocabulary.meaning: the part of a garment that covers some or all of a person's arm
vocabulary.example: He rolled up his sleeves and started working.

vocabulary.meaning: having an even and regular surface; free from perceptible projections or roughness
vocabulary.example: The marble floor was smooth and shiny.

vocabulary.meaning: to pull something to make it longer or wider
vocabulary.example: Stretch the rubber band as far as it can go.

vocabulary.meaning: marked with long, narrow bands of different color or texture
vocabulary.example: He wore a black and white striped shirt.

vocabulary.meaning: a particular design, shape, or type of something
vocabulary.example: She likes to try different styles of clothing.

vocabulary.meaning: to be convenient for or go well with, or a set of outer clothes made of the same fabric
vocabulary.example: That haircut really suits you. / He wore a dark blue suit to the job interview.

vocabulary.meaning: right or appropriate for a particular person, purpose, or situation
vocabulary.example: This movie is not suitable for young children.

vocabulary.meaning: to pull apart or into pieces by force, or a hole or split caused by tearing
vocabulary.example: Be careful not to tear the paper. / There was a small tear in the sleeve of his jacket.

vocabulary.meaning: fitting closely around your body or a particular object
vocabulary.example: These shoes are too tight and hurt my feet.

vocabulary.meaning: a device or implement, especially one held in the hand, used to carry out a particular function
vocabulary.example: A hammer is a very useful tool for driving nails.

vocabulary.meaning: a large animal with a long neck and one or two humps on its back, used in deserts
vocabulary.example: We rode camels during our trip to the Sahara desert.

vocabulary.meaning: a heavy building material made from a mixture of stone, sand, cement, and water, or specific/definite
vocabulary.example: The building has a solid concrete foundation. / Can you give me a concrete example of this rule?

vocabulary.meaning: literary work in which special intensity is given to the expression of feelings and ideas by the use of distinctive style and rhythm
vocabulary.example: She enjoys reading and writing romantic poetry.

vocabulary.meaning: a large, tall cabinet in which clothes may be hung or stored
vocabulary.example: He hung his new coats in the wardrobe.

phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She cut off a piece of fabric to make a pocket.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to fasten or button up clothing
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Could you help me do up the zip at the back of my dress?
EX: He filled up the car with petrol before the road trip.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to wear clothing or an accessory
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She had a beautiful gold necklace on.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to exclude or omit someone/something from a group or list
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You can leave out the last paragraph if the essay is too long.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to dress oneself in clothing or footwear
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Put on your coat; it's very cold outside.
EX: Please take off your shoes before entering the house.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to put on a garment to see if it fits and looks good
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You should try on the jeans before buying them.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: at the rear part of something
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The garden is at the back of the house.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: at the final part of something
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: We are planning to go on holiday at the end of July.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: popular and trendy at a particular time
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Striped shirts are in fashion this summer.
EX: There is a beautiful fountain in front of the building.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: at the place where two lines or walls meet
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: He sat quietly in the corner of the cafe.
EX: Flared trousers went out of fashion many years ago.

wordFormation.meaning: artist: a person who creates paintings or drawings / artistic: having or revealing natural creative skill
wordFormation.example: EX: Picasso is a very famous artist. / She has a lot of artistic talent.

wordFormation.meaning: break-broke-broken: separate into pieces / (un)breakable: easy or impossible to break
wordFormation.example: EX: Be careful not to break the vase. / These plastic glasses are unbreakable.

wordFormation.meaning: freedom: the power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants
wordFormation.example: EX: The prisoners fought for their freedom.

wordFormation.meaning: exhibition: a public display of works of art or items of interest
wordFormation.example: EX: We visited a modern art exhibition yesterday.

wordFormation.meaning: composition: a work of music, literature, or art / composer: a person who writes music
wordFormation.example: EX: Mozart wrote many famous musical compositions. / He is a talented classical composer.

wordFormation.meaning: handful: a quantity that fills the hand / handle: the part by which a tool, bag, or cup is held
wordFormation.example: EX: He ate a handful of nuts. / The handle of the suitcase was broken.

wordFormation.meaning: imagination: the faculty or action of forming new ideas or images / imaginative: having or showing creativity or inventiveness
wordFormation.example: EX: Children have a very vivid imagination. / The book is full of imaginative stories.

wordFormation.meaning: intelligence: the ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills
wordFormation.example: EX: Artificial intelligence is developing rapidly.

wordFormation.meaning: perfection: the state of being perfect / imperfect: not perfect; faulty
wordFormation.example: EX: She strives for perfection in everything she does. / The diamond has a few minor imperfect details.

wordFormation.meaning: preparation: the action or process of making ready for use or consideration
wordFormation.example: EX: Proper preparation is key to passing the test.

wordPatterns.example: EX: She was amazed by the magician's tricks.

wordPatterns.meaning: sad or displeased because expectations were not met
wordPatterns.example: EX: He was disappointed with his test results.

wordPatterns.meaning: having good knowledge or understanding of
wordPatterns.example: EX: Are you familiar with the rules of chess?

wordPatterns.meaning: taking part in or actively associated with
wordPatterns.example: EX: Many local residents were involved in the community project.

wordPatterns.meaning: having a resemblance in appearance, character, or quantity
wordPatterns.example: EX: His jacket is very similar to mine.

wordPatterns.meaning: to transform or make something different
wordPatterns.example: EX: The fairy changed the pumpkin into a carriage.

wordPatterns.meaning: to depict or represent something in a particular way
wordPatterns.example: EX: She described the movie as exciting and fun.

wordPatterns.meaning: to make something clear by describing it in more detail
wordPatterns.example: EX: Can you explain the homework to me?

wordPatterns.meaning: to cause someone to remember someone or something
wordPatterns.example: EX: This song reminds me of my childhood holidays.

wordPatterns.meaning: to take something away from a place
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please remove your books from the dining table.

wordPatterns.meaning: the capacity to have an effect on character or development
wordPatterns.example: EX: Peer pressure can have a strong influence on teenagers.

wordPatterns.meaning: a painting, drawing, or photograph depicting something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She has a picture of her family on her desk.
